Buffalo mass-shooting suspect may pursue psychiatric defense, lawyers say   Lawyers for the alleged gunman may have little room to argue that mental illness was a factor, because evidence of planning and deliberate conduct tends to weigh heavily against psychiatric defenses

Whistleblower says Patrick Brown arranged her payment by third-party company   Debra Jodoin says Brown told her 'it was permissible ... to be employed by a company as a consultant, and then for that company to have me volunteer with the campaign'
Russia is just getting started in Ukraine, Putin tells ministers   In a hawkish speech to parliamentary leaders, Putin also dares West to try to defeat it on the battlefield

A Fox Sports personality got called out for suggesting Black NBA players don't like Toronto   Chris Broussard said he doesn't think Kevin Durant will go to Toronto. 'It’s not America. And you feel it when you’re there ... especially as an African American'

Allister Heath: Big spending Boris betrayed supporters, made mockery of Brexit   None of the good things have been delivered, and all of the bad ones have happened, and worse

10/3 podcast: What it takes to make Conservative Party a contender again   As candidates duke it out over who is best to lead the Conservative Party of Canada, many in the conservative movement are asking what the party itself should look like, and how to best position itself to beat the Liberals in the next election.

As airport chaos continues, no sign of Liberal government's promised consumer advocate   Government documents show that as of a year ago, Innovation, Science and Economic Development was still working on a mandate for the Canadian Consumer Advocate
Chris Selley: Patrick Brown is the inevitable product of broken leadership-selection process   The days of caucus alone electing its leader are likely gone forever. But it should at least have a say

Patrick Brown intends to appeal disqualification from Conservative leadership race: lawyers   'This Kafkaesque process led to a politically motivated and preordained result and is not consistent with the values that should be upheld by this party'

'Everybody' thought allegations against Patrick Brown were 'serious': Party source   The committee wanted Elections Canada to investigate, but some wanted to wait until that investigation was done before deciding on Brown's fate, a source said
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

Cook this: Pollo al pastor — spicy-sweet chipotle-roast chicken — from Mi Cocina   'It's one of those dishes that makes everybody smile because it smells so good,' says Rick Martínez
Scott Stinson: What the Toronto Raptors would have to give up for Kevin Durant is not what matters most   Trying to figure out what Durant wants has always been a bit like decoding hieroglyphics
Cook this: Balinese green bean urap from The Nutmeg Trail   'It is a true vegetal celebration and is particularly good served alongside chicken sate,' says Eleanor Ford
Daily Horoscope for Thursday, July 7   Avoid shopping or important decisions after 9 PM EDT today (from 6 PM to 10:30 PM PDT). After 10:30 PM PDT, the Moon moves from Libra into Scorpio
Daily Horoscope for Wednesday, July 6   There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The Moon is in Libra
Cook this: Poc chuc — orange- and lime-marinated grilled pork — from Mi Cocina   'The first thing that drew me to this dish is how beautiful it is,' says Rick Martínez
Cook this: Aguachile from Mi Cocina   'It's such a beautifully simple dish,' says Rick Martínez of this iconic Mazatlán morning meal
Senior Living: A message to our millennials   Determination and ingenuity will help you surmount challenges
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.
U.S. agency probing self-driving Cruise car crash in California   Vehicle operating in autonomous-drive mode involved in collision in San Francisco that caused minor injuries
Volkswagen shows off the Phaeton successor it never built   The handsome top-line sedan was killed off as the automaker turned to electrification instead
This Stellantis designer wants to remove all screens from car interiors   Because a black screen covered in fingerprints isn’t very “sexy,” is it?
Most American drivers interested in shopping EVs: study   And unfamiliarity with them seems to be a major factor for those who aren't, says the Consumer Reports survey
36-year-old driver hauled from flaming wreckage on the QEW   Bystanders leap into action while car burns

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.
Clint Eastwood handed $2.6 million after winning a second trademark infringement CBD lawsuit   Company did not have permission to use his image or likeness.
